codeReview.summary: The commit 56b2d9e only renamed the test module from 'tests' to 'episodic' - no actual feature implementation. The worker claimed the feature was 'already implemented' but the log_observation() convenience method is MISSING from EventLog.

issues.description: log_observation() method is missing. EventType::Observation and ObservationPayload are defined but no convenience method exists to log observations. The feature description requires 'observation' to be stored as episodic memory and expectedBehavior states 'Stores observations and outcomes immutably' - but there's no way to log observations via the public API.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker claimed in handoff that 'The EventLog struct provides methods like log_tool_invocation(), log_decision(), log_observation(), log_error(), log_outcome()' - but log_observation() does NOT exist in the codebase. The worker made a false statement about the implementation state.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff states log_observation() exists, but crates/swell-memory/src/event_log.rs EventLog impl block (lines 148-309) has no log_observation() method. Only log_tool_invocation, log_decision, log_error, log_outcome, log_state_transition are present.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The Rust skill was invoked but worker did not thoroughly verify the API surface - they claimed the feature was 'already implemented' without checking if all documented methods existed.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript shows worker read event_log.rs but concluded implementation was complete without verifying log_observation() existed.

summary: FAIL - The log_observation() convenience method is missing from EventLog. Commit 56b2d9e only renamed the test module - no actual implementation was added. The feature cannot fulfill its contract to 'store observations immutably' without this method.
